tutorial.title: How to Play
tutorial.subtitle: Master the art of geographical guessing in just a few steps.
tutorial.step1Desc: You'll be dropped into a random street-level panorama somewhere in the world. Use your mouse or touch to look around and explore your surroundings for clues.
tutorial.step2Desc: Pay attention to road signs, language on storefronts, vegetation, architecture, driving side, and terrain. Every detail can help you narrow down your location.
tutorial.step3Desc: Open the mini-map and click where you think you are. The closer your guess is to the actual location, the more points you earn.
tutorial.step4Desc: After each round, see how close you were. In multiplayer, compare your score against friends on the live leaderboard.
tutorial.step5Desc: Choose from Time Limit, Distance Limit, or Elimination mode. Each adds a unique twist to the challenge.
tutorial.tip1: Sun position can help determine hemisphere — shadows point north in the Southern Hemisphere.
tutorial.tip2: Google coverage cars often have visible shadows or reflections. Mapillary images have different camera rigs.
tutorial.tip3: Road markings and sign styles vary by country — learn the common patterns.
tutorial.tip4: Vegetation zones (tropical, arid, temperate) can quickly narrow your region.
tutorial.tip5: Language on signs is your strongest clue — learn to recognize scripts and alphabets.

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.subtitle: Please read these terms carefully before using ranzaGG.
terms.s1Desc: By accessing and using ranzaGG, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Use. If you do not agree, please do not use our service.
terms.s2Desc: ranzaGG is a free, ad-free geographical guessing game powered by Mapillary street-level imagery. The service is provided as-is for entertainment and educational purposes.
terms.s3Desc: You agree not to use the service for any unlawful purpose, attempt to disrupt or overload the service, exploit bugs or vulnerabilities, or impersonate other users.
terms.s4Desc: Street-level imagery is provided by Mapillary and is subject to their terms of service and licensing. The ranzaGG name, logo, and game mechanics are the property of the ranzaGG team.
terms.s5Desc: We do not collect personal data beyond what is necessary for gameplay (e.g., temporary usernames stored in session). No cookies are used for tracking. No ads are displayed.
terms.s6Desc: ranzaGG is provided without warranty of any kind. We are not responsible for the accuracy or availability of street-level imagery. The service may be modified or discontinued at any time.
terms.s7Desc: We reserve the right to modify these terms at any time. Continued use of the service after changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms.

about.title: About ranzaGG
about.subtitle: A free and open-source GeoGuessr alternative for everyone.
about.whatDesc: ranzaGG is a 100% free, ad-free geographical guessing game. Explore street-level panoramas from around the world and guess where you are. Play solo or challenge your friends in real-time multiplayer matches.
about.feature1: Completely free — no ads, no subscriptions, no paywalls.
about.feature2: Singleplayer and real-time multiplayer with lobby system.
about.feature3: Multiple game modes: Time Limit, Distance Limit, Elimination.
about.feature4: Filter by continent, country, or city for focused gameplay.
about.feature5: Powered by Mapillary's crowd-sourced street-level imagery.
about.feature6: Dark theme with customizable map styles.
about.techDesc: Built with Nuxt 3, Vue 3, Socket.IO for real-time multiplayer, Mapillary JS for panoramas, and Leaflet for interactive maps. The entire codebase is open-source.
about.credit2: Map tiles by CARTO and OpenStreetMap contributors.
